transcript:
Agent: NetFast support, Tom speaking. How can I help?

Customer: Hi Tom. My internet has been completely down since 6 AM this morning. I work from home and I have missed two important meetings. This is really impacting my job.

Agent: I am sorry about that, Priya. Let me check your line right away. I can see there is an active outage affecting your area — it is a node failure on the infrastructure side.

Customer: When will it be fixed? I cannot afford to keep missing meetings.

Agent: Our engineers are currently working on it. The estimated resolution time is 6 PM today. I know that is a long time and I apologize.

Customer: That is not acceptable. I need internet now. Can you at least give me a credit?

Agent: Absolutely. I will apply a full day credit of $3.20 to your account. I know that does not solve the problem today but it is the right thing to do.

Customer: What about a temporary solution? Can I use mobile data somehow?

Agent: I can suggest using your mobile phone as a hotspot if your plan supports it, but I cannot set that up on your behalf as it would be through your mobile carrier. Unfortunately there is no other immediate solution I can offer for the infrastructure issue.

Customer: So there is nothing else you can do?

Agent: For today's outage, I am afraid not — it is an area-wide issue. I have logged your complaint as priority and you will receive an automated SMS when service is restored. I am truly sorry we cannot get this fixed sooner.

Customer: This is really disappointing. I hope this does not happen again.

Agent: I completely understand. The credit will show on your next bill, and our team is doing everything possible to restore service by 6 PM.
